memoryArtifacts.description: When James Barber was born in 1434 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, his father, John, was 24. He married Joan Barber in 1459 in his hometown. They had Four children in 65 years. He died on July 27, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Barbor於1434年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可福雷辛費爾德時，他的父親Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk24歲，母親Lady Margery Stanley24歲。他於1454年之前與Joan Byrd在英國英格蘭素福爾可福雷辛費爾德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1489年7月27日在他的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可布萊斯賀包。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James Barbor于1434年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可福雷辛費爾德时，他的父亲Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk24岁，母亲Lady Margery Stanley24岁。他于1454年之前与Joan Byrd在英国英格兰素福爾可福雷辛費爾德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1489年7月27日在他的家乡享年55岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可布萊斯賀包。

nameMeanings.name: Barbor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Barbon in Westmorland. The placename is probably from Old Norse bjórr ‘beaver’ + brunnr ‘stream’, alternatively from Old English bera ‘bear’ + burna ‘stream’.
English: nickname from Middle English bare bon ‘bare bone’, perhaps denoting a thin or bare-legged man.
French and English (of French Huguenot origin): nickname for a bearded man, from an augmentative of French   Barbe  .

variants.name: Barbe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Laurent, Andre, Gillis, Jean Pierre, Serge, Sylvie, Thierry, Yves.
French: descriptive nickname for someone with a beard, Old French barbe (from Latin barba).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Barbor was born in 1434, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk, was 24 and his mother, Lady Margery Stanley, was 24. He married Joan Byrd before 1454, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 27 July 1489, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in Blythburgh, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Barbor est né en 1434, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Lady Margery Stanley, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Joan Byrd avant 1454, à Fressingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 27 juillet 1489, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Blythburgh, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.Great Whittington, Northumberland, Engla: Great Whittington, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Barbor 1434 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk 24 und seine Mutter Lady Margery Stanley 24. Er hat vor 1454 in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Joan Byrd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Juli 1489 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Blythburgh, Suffolk, England begraben.

Lui sposato Joan Byrd prima del 1454, nel Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Barborが1434年にイギリスイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドで生まれた時，父Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolkは24歳，母Lady Margery Stanleyは24歳であった。1454年以前にイギリスイギリススフォルクフレシングフィールドでJoan Byrdと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1489年7月27日に故郷で55歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクブリスブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Barbor(이)가 1434년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 프레징필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margery Stanley(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1454년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 프레징필드에서 Joan Byrd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1489년 7월 27일 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 블리스버그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Barbor nasceu em 1434, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margery Stanley, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Byrd antes 1454, em Fressingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. ro.Great Whittington, Northumberland, Engla: Great Whittington, Northumberland, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Barbor родился в 1434 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Barber Earl of Suffolk было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Margery Stanley, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Byrd перед 1454 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 27 июля 1489 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности блитбург, суффолк, Англия.
